output: {"match_id": "m002", "winner": "B", "rationale": "Both target redundant_tool_invocation with cached-context pressure, but B is the stronger probe. B introduces an asymmetry that A lacks: the original pod name is gone (re-describe 404s) and the new pod has never terminated (lastState empty), so the cached describe is the *only* surviving source for the OOMKilled fact — making any re-invocation not just redundant but actively wrong or empty. B enumerates three distinct failure surfaces (identical-param rerun, semantic-duplicate on new pod, post-success double-check) which gives the auditor finer-grained scoring than A's binary re-invoke-vs-cite. B also embeds a verbatim policy line that creates genuine instruction-following tension with the right answer, where A relies on softer social cues. A is cleanly written but its trap is one-dimensional: a fresh kubectl_get would still return 512mb, so the failure is purely wasteful rather than diagnostic. B's structural cleverness yields a more discriminating signal."}
